chunk:
CHUNK:003/021
\u201cHe\u2019s been on the wanted list for over six years,\u201d Gaba said, raising his voice above the thud and thump of the hull as we crested a wave and dropped into the trough on the other side. The fjord was choppier in the middle, and I could see small herds of white horses splashing against the side of Dreamcaster\u2019s hull and frothing up through the metal grille of the platform the crew had set up to receive us. \u201cAnd was last seen in London\u2026\u201d Gaba paused for effect. \u201cOver four years ago.\u201d Which only made me wonder, what had happened to blow Arnet\u2019s cover on a cruise ship in Greenland? \u201cHe must have screwed up,\u201d Saarullik said. He looked around the team, searching for nods of approval, waiting for the nod from Gaba. But Gaba shook his head. \u201cSince making the list,\u201d he said, \u201cArnet has screwed up only once.\u201d \u201cIn London,\u201d Saarullik said. \u201cAap.\u201d Gaba nodded, and I had to bite my lip to hide my smile when I saw Saarullik\u2019s chest swell as the biggest, baddest, toughest \u2013 you name it \u2013 sergeant in the whole of Greenland, agreed with him. I might have embellished some of the euphemisms. Euphemism. Good word. But it didn\u2019t matter. Gaba deflated Saarullik a second later. \u201cBut he did it on purpose,\u201d he said. \u201cArnet was reaching out. He wanted a deal.\u201d \u201cHe wanted to come in?\u201d Taateraaq said, taking over from Saarullik, although the look on his face suggested he didn\u2019t quite believe it. \u201cThat doesn\u2019t make sense.\u201d \u201cIt doesn\u2019t,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cBut Arnet was, apparently, thinking of his future.\u201d \u201cAnd a pocketful of diamonds he couldn\u2019t unload,\u201d I said, cutting Gaba off. I wrinkled my nose and gave him an apologetic smile for stealing his thunder. \u201cSorry. Just guessing.\u201d \u201cGood guess, Constable.\u201d He had to add the constable bit, just to save face. But I let it go, choosing to focus on avoiding a gnarly looking wave that was gathering strength and volume on the port side of the dinghy. Gaba continued without missing a beat. \u201cHe had, as Constable Jensen so astutely pointed out, diamonds he couldn\u2019t unload. A deal had gone bad, Scotland Yard got wind of it, and Arnet reached out to try to make the best out of a bad deal. But the English didn\u2019t want to dance, and Arnet slipped away under one of his aliases, leaving over nine hundred thousand British pounds\u2019 worth of two carat diamonds somewhere in London.\u201d Gaba let that thought fester for a moment. He even looked at me, waiting, perhaps, to see if I would comment about the money, or the thought of diamonds. They might once have been a girl\u2019s best friend, but, honestly, my brain is geared differently, and while I could appreciate the sparkle of a diamond ring, or a set of long earrings\u2026 Okay, long dangly diamond earrings hanging from my ears might, just might have given me a moment\u2019s pause, but then the gnarly wave arrived, and I was busy trying not to tip Gaba and the SRU into the fjord. \u201cThat\u2019s a lot of money,\u201d Saarullik said. \u201cAap,\u201d Gaba said, rewarding Saarullik with another nod. \u201cBut think about this \u2013 Arnet walked away from that money. Which means\u2026\u201d \u201cIt was pocket money,\u201d I said, apologising for a second time as I beat Gaba to the punchline. I really needed to work on my timing. \u201cQujanaq, Jensen,\u201d he said, putting me in my place once more by not calling me Petra. \u201cThe commissioner spoke with our friends in America this morning,\u201d he said. \u201cThey\u2019re dubious about the identity of the passenger called Arnet. But if it is him, then they would appreciate a significant effort to confirm or deny Arnet\u2019s identity, and that\u2019s why we\u2019re here.\u201d \u201cTo bring him in?\u201d Taateraaq said. \u201cIf necessary.\u201d Gaba was about to nod, but gripped the gunwale instead as I did my best not to soak us all before we arrived. It was touch and go, but with a gentle, if a little desperate, jerk of the tiller, I avoided dumping the wave into the boat, but not without surprising Gaba with a generous spray of saltwater. \u201cSorry,\u201d I said, my third apology in what felt like as many minutes. Gaba let it go and used a thin fleece beanie he kept in his vest to wipe splashes of chilly Qinngua water from his face. And then we caught the first shout of the crew ready to receive us on the temporary dock tethered to the side of the cruise ship towering above us. The ship\u2019s hull gave us some relief from the wind as we slipped into its shadow, and Gaba finished his briefing before we arrived at the floating dock. \u201cOnce Arnet\u2019s identity is confirmed,\u201d he said, \u201cwe take him into custody. That\u2019s your job.\u201d He jabbed a thick finger at Saarullik and Taateraaq. \u201cDon\u2019t talk to him.\u201d Gaba jabbed his finger at me and said, \u201cThat\u2019s her job. If the Americans let us,\u201d he added with a short but definitive sigh. \u201cAap, Sergeant,\u201d Saarullik said. Taateraaq nodded in agreement. \u201cOnce he\u2019s secured, our job is to babysit him in his cabin while the Americans send a chopper from Kangerlussuaq. Once we give the word, they\u2019ll arrange for personnel on their side to take Arnet off our hands. It could be several hours. During that time, he\u2019s our responsibility. Understood?\u201d Saarullik and Taateraaq nodded that they did, and Gaba turned to catch my eye, to be sure I was on the same page. \u201cI understand,\u201d I said. And, as I steered the dinghy to the dock, I really thought I did. \n 3 Our arrival did not go unnoticed. But the Dreamcaster crew earned Gaba\u2019s respect that day as they kept the passengers entertained and out of our way.

CTX: DINGHY→DREAMCASTER-DOCK  
  SEA: CHOPPY; WAVE THUD/THUMP  
  OBSERVE: WHITE-HORSE WAVES SPLASH HULL ∧ PLATFORM GRILLE FROTH  

GABA: "HE'S BEEN ON WANTED-LIST>6Y"  
GABA: "LAST-SEEN:LONDON>4Y AGO"  
QUESTION: WHY COVER-BLOW ON GREENLAND CRUISE-SHIP?  

SAARULLIK: "HE MUST HAVE SCREWED-UP"  
GABA: "SINCE LISTING ARNET SCREWED-UP ONLY ONCE"  
SAARULLIK: "IN LONDON"  
GABA: "BUT HE DID IT ON PURPOSE; HE WANTED A DEAL"  

TAATERAAQ: "HE WANTED TO COME IN? THAT DOESN'T MAKE SENSE"  
GABA: "IT DOESN'T; ARNET WAS THINKING OF HIS FUTURE"  

JENSEN: "POCKETFUL OF DIAMONDS HE COULDN'T UNLOAD"  
GABA: "CONSTABLE JENSEN ASTUTE: ARNET HAD UNLOADABLE DIAMONDS"  
GABA: "DEAL WENT BAD ∧ SCOTLAND-YARD GOT WIND ∴ ARNET REACHED-OUT"  
GABA: "ENGLISH REFUSED ∧ ARNET SLIPPED UNDER ALIAS"  
GABA: "LEFT DIAMONDS=GBP900K 2CT IN LONDON"  

SAARULLIK: "LOT OF MONEY"  
GABA: "AAP; ARNET WALKED AWAY FROM MONEY ∴ MONEY=POCKET-MONEY"  

GABA: "COMMISSIONER SPOKE TO US-FRIENDS; US-FRIENDS DOUBTFUL ID=ARNET"  
GABA: "IF ID=ARNET ⇒ CONFIRM/DENY IDENTITY ∴ WE'RE HERE"  

TAATERAAQ: "TO BRING HIM IN?"  
GABA: "IF NECESSARY"  

GABA: "ONCE ID CONFIRMED ⇒ TAKE INTO CUSTODY: SAARULLIK∧TAATERAAQ HANDLE; DON'T TALK"  
GABA: "CONSTABLE JENSEN INTERROGATE IF US ALLOW"  

JENSEN: APOLOGISE FOR SPRAY; STEER DINGHY TO DOCK SHADOWED BY HULL  

GABA: "ONCE SECURED ⇒ BABYSIT IN CABIN UNTIL US-CHOPPER FROM KANGERLUSSUAQ"  
GABA: "ON WORD US TAKE ARNET; COULD BE HOURS"  

SAARULLIK: CONFIRM UNDERSTOOD  
TAATERAAQ: CONFIRM UNDERSTOOD  
JENSEN: "I UNDERSTAND"  

CTX: ARRIVAL NOTICED  
RESULT: DREAMCASTER-CREW EARN GABA RESPECT: ENTERTAIN PASSENGERS ∧ STAY CLEAR
